key_insight: OmniMatBench shows that current multimodal language models still struggle to reason across the breadth of materials science, with the best model scoring only 0.372 overall.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark helps identify where AI assistants can and cannot support materials research workflows, from knowledge retrieval to application-level reasoning.
method: Built a human-calibrated multimodal benchmark with 3,171 expert-curated QA and calculation problems.
Covers 19 materials-science subfields spanning fundamental, structural, processing, and functional materials.
Evaluated 13 open-source and closed-source MLLMs under formula-, retrieval-, and code-assisted settings.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 3,171 expert-curated problems across 19 subfields and an overall best-model score of 0.372, indicating a large reasoning gap.
watch_item: Performance varies strongly by subfield, so benchmark scores may not reflect reliable real-world usefulness in specialized materials tasks.
paper_title: OmniMatBench: A Human-Calibrated Multimodal Reasoning Benchmark Across 19 Materials Science Subfields
